job_statement: When deploying applications for multiple teams, I want to understand multitenancy architecture and instance scopes, so I can choose the right deployment model for my organization.
related_jobs: Deploy Argo CD instances; Configure RBAC for tenants
desired_outcomes: Minimize risk of tenant interference; Reduce complexity of permission management; Ensure appropriate isolation between teams
notes: Main job covering architecture understanding and planning multitenancy strategy.

job_statement: When providing GitOps capabilities to application teams, I want to deploy namespace-scoped Argo CD instances, so I can give teams autonomy without granting cluster-admin privileges.
related_jobs: Extend instance permissions to multiple namespaces; Configure custom cluster roles
desired_outcomes: Minimize security risk from privilege escalation; Reduce administrative overhead for tenant requests; Ensure tenants can self-manage their instances
notes: Core job for setting up isolated tenant instances. Multiple approaches via labels and environment variables.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when enabling a namespace-scoped instance to deploy to multiple namespaces, I want to use the argocd.argoproj.io/managed-by label, so I can automatically provision required roles and bindings.
prerequisites: Have namespace-scoped Argo CD instance deployed; Identify target namespaces for management
related_jobs: Define custom cluster roles for controllers
desired_outcomes: Minimize manual RBAC configuration; Reduce likelihood of permission errors; Ensure consistent role provisioning across namespaces
notes: Label-based approach for namespace management. Only works for same-cluster namespaces.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when default admin permissions are too permissive, I want to define custom cluster roles via CONTROLLER_CLUSTER_ROLE and CONTROLLER_SERVER_ROLE environment variables, so I can enforce least-privilege access for Argo CD controllers.
prerequisites: Understand required Argo CD permissions; Define custom cluster roles
desired_outcomes: Minimize excessive permissions granted to controllers; Reduce security risk from overly broad roles; Ensure controllers can still function with restricted permissions
notes: Advanced security configuration for fine-grained permission control.

job_statement: When managing cluster-level resources or supporting Applications in any namespace, I want to deploy a cluster-scoped Argo CD instance, so I can manage resources across the entire cluster.
prerequisites: Understand security implications of cluster-scoped instances; Identify use cases requiring cluster-level access
desired_outcomes: Minimize risk of unauthorized cluster-wide access; Reduce fragmentation of cluster configuration management; Ensure appropriate permissions for cluster-level operations
notes: For cluster configuration use cases. Critical security considerations for elevation.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I need to grant cluster-level permissions to an Argo CD instance, I want to use the ARGOCD_CLUSTER_CONFIG_NAMESPACES environment variable in the Subscription resource, so I can prevent non-admins from self-elevating their instances.
parent_job: Deploy a cluster-scoped Argo CD instance
prerequisites: Verify instance is not self-managed by tenants; Modify GitOps Operator Subscription resource
related_jobs: Create additional cluster roles for extended permissions
desired_outcomes: Minimize risk of privilege escalation; Reduce likelihood of unauthorized cluster access; Ensure only authorized instances have cluster-admin capabilities
notes: Security control preventing tenant self-elevation to cluster-admin.

job_statement: When managing cluster configuration resources, I want to use the default openshift-gitops instance, so I can maintain a centralized control plane for cluster-level operations.
related_jobs: Create separate instances for tenant application delivery
desired_outcomes: Minimize complexity by centralizing cluster configuration; Reduce risk by separating cluster management from application delivery; Ensure default instance is not misused for tenant workloads
notes: Default instance is purpose-built for cluster config only. Explicitly NOT for application delivery.

job_statement: When designing multitenancy solutions, I want to understand the two-level RBAC model (Kubernetes and Argo CD), so I can mitigate privilege escalation risks.
prerequisites: Understand Kubernetes RBAC; Understand Argo CD permission model
related_jobs: Configure Argo CD projects with restrictions; Use separate Argo CD instances for isolation
desired_outcomes: Minimize risk of tenants escalating privileges; Reduce likelihood of unauthorized resource access; Ensure proper separation between Kubernetes and Argo CD permissions
notes: Critical security concept for multitenancy. Two distinct permission layers must be understood.

job_statement: When managing multiple tenants with an Argo CD instance, I want to define AppProject custom resources with granular RBAC and restrictions, so I can control what resources tenants can deploy and where.
prerequisites: Understand Argo CD projects concept; Identify tenant resource and namespace requirements
related_jobs: Configure global RBAC in Argo CD CR; Use global projects for common configuration
desired_outcomes: Minimize RBAC configuration duplication; Reduce risk of tenants deploying to unauthorized namespaces; Ensure project-level restrictions are enforced
notes: Best practice: define AppProject CRs rather than global RBAC. Never use default project.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when managing many tenants with common configuration needs, I want to define global projects for shared configuration, so I can reduce duplication and maintenance overhead.
parent_job: Define AppProject custom resources for tenant management
prerequisites: Identify common tenant project configurations; Define global projects
related_jobs: Inherit global projects in tenant projects
desired_outcomes: Minimize configuration duplication across tenants; Reduce maintenance burden for common settings; Ensure consistent policy enforcement
notes: Optimization for large tenant counts with shared configuration patterns.

section: Enable tenant namespace management with NamespaceManagement CR
job_statement: When enabling tenant self-service for namespace management, I want to use the NamespaceManagement CR feature, so I can allow tenants to delegate control without requiring cluster-admin intervention.
prerequisites: Enable ALLOW_NAMESPACE_MANAGEMENT_IN_NAMESPACE_SCOPED_INSTANCES in Subscription; Configure Argo CD CR with namespace management patterns
related_jobs: Configure glob patterns for namespace matching; Monitor NamespaceManagement CR lifecycle
desired_outcomes: Minimize cluster-admin toil for namespace delegation; Reduce delay in tenant namespace provisioning; Ensure secure and automated RBAC provisioning
evidence: multitenancy-combined.adoc -> Enable tenant namespace management, lines 119-133
notes: Self-service feature disabled by default for security. Requires explicit cluster-admin enablement.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when configuring namespace management, I want to use glob patterns to match multiple namespaces, so I can enable tenants to self-delegate without individual approvals.
parent_job: Enable tenant self-service for namespace management
prerequisites: Enable namespace management feature in Subscription; Define namespace naming conventions
related_jobs: Monitor automatic RBAC provisioning; Update cluster secrets
desired_outcomes: Minimize manual namespace approval overhead; Reduce time to provision tenant namespaces; Ensure consistent RBAC provisioning across namespaces
notes: Pattern-based approach enables scalable tenant self-service. Example patterns: /*, tenant-/*.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when understanding NamespaceManagement CR lifecycle, I want to know the operator's automated responses to creation/update/deletion, so I can predict RBAC provisioning behavior.
parent_job: Enable tenant self-service for namespace management
related_jobs: Monitor Role and RoleBinding creation; Verify cluster secret updates
desired_outcomes: Minimize unexpected RBAC state; Reduce troubleshooting time for permission issues; Ensure predictable cleanup on CR deletion
evidence: multitenancy-combined.adoc -> Effects of NamespaceManagement CR, lines 145-153
notes: Lifecycle awareness critical for troubleshooting. Automatic cleanup on Subscription-level disable.

section: Configuring namespace management for Argo CD tenants
job_statement: As a tenant user, when I need my Argo CD instance to manage my namespaces, I want to create a NamespaceManagement CR in my namespace, so I can delegate control without waiting for cluster-admin approval.
parent_job: Enable tenant self-service for namespace management
prerequisites: Cluster administrator has enabled namespace management; Cluster administrator has configured namespace patterns in Argo CD CR; Have permissions to create NamespaceManagement CR in namespace
related_jobs: Verify Role and RoleBinding creation; Deploy applications to managed namespaces
desired_outcomes: Minimize delay in namespace delegation; Reduce dependency on cluster-admin for namespace setup; Ensure automatic RBAC provisioning completes successfully
notes: Tenant perspective on self-service namespace management. Requires three-step procedure: enable feature, configure Argo CD CR, create NamespaceManagement CR.
